title: Sarga 41
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “And the mighty-armed Rama, having dismissed the bears, monkeys, and Rakshasas, was very happy and rejoiced greatly along with his brothers.” and develops toward “If it is so, then welcome to you, O best of aerial cars, Pushpaka; due to the favor of Kubera, no impropriety shall befall us.”

This passage begins with “Then, the mighty-armed Rama worshipped the Pushpaka chariot with parched grains, flowers, and fragrant incense.” and develops toward “Having heard these very sweet words spoken by Bharata, Rama, the best of kings, became filled with joy. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये…”

verses.sanskrit: विसृज्य च महाबाहुर्ऋक्षवानरराक्षसान् । भ्रातृभिः सहितो रामः प्रमुमोद सुखं सुखी ।। 7.41.1 ।।
verses.translation: And the mighty-armed Rama, having dismissed the bears, monkeys, and Rakshasas, was very happy and rejoiced greatly along with his brothers.

verses.sanskrit: अथापराह्णसमये भ्रातृभिः सह राघवः । शुश्राव मधुरां वाणीमन्तरिक्षात् प्रभाषिताम् ।। 7.41.2 ।।
verses.translation: Then, in the afternoon, Rama, along with his brothers, heard a sweet voice spoken from the sky.

verses.sanskrit: सौम्य राम निरीक्षस्व सौम्येन वदनेन माम् । कुबेरभवनात्प्राप्तं विद्धि मां पुष्पकं प्रभो ।। 7.41.3 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle Rama, look at me with a gentle face; know me, O Lord, to be the Pushpaka chariot that has arrived from Kubera's abode.

verses.sanskrit: तव शासनमाज्ञाय गतो ऽस्मि भवनं प्रति । उपस्थातुं नरश्रेष्ठ स च मां प्रत्यभाषत ।। 7.41.4 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, having understood your command, I went to his abode to serve, and he (Kubera) spoke to me in return.

verses.sanskrit: निर्जितस्त्वं नरेन्द्रेण राघवेण महात्मना । निहत्य युधि दुर्धर्षं रावणं राक्षसेश्वरम् ।। 7.41.5 ।।
verses.translation: He said, "You have been conquered by the great-souled king Rama, the descendant of Raghu, who slew the formidable Ravana, the lord of Rakshasas, in battle."

verses.sanskrit: ममापि परमा प्रीतर्हते तस्मिन्दुरात्मनि । रावणे सगणे चैव सपुत्रे सहबान्धवे ।। 7.41.6 ।।
verses.translation: He further said, "My supreme joy also arises from the killing of that evil-minded Ravana, along with his retinue, sons, and relatives."

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं रामेण लङ्कायां निर्जितः परमात्मना । वह सौम्य तमेव त्वमहमाज्ञापयामि ते ।। 7.41.7 ।।
verses.translation: He concluded, "Therefore, O gentle Pushpaka, since you were conquered by the supreme Rama in Lanka, you must carry him; I command you to do so."

verses.sanskrit: परमो ह्येष मे कामो यत्त्वं राघवनन्दनम् । वहेर्लोकस्य संयानं गच्छस्व विगतज्वरः ।। 7.41.8 ।।
verses.translation: This is indeed my greatest desire, that you should carry Rama and the people on their journey; therefore, go forth without any anxiety.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं शासनमाज्ञाय धनदस्य महात्मनः । त्वत्सकाशमनुप्राप्तो निर्विशङ्कः प्रतीक्ष माम् ।। 7.41.9 ।।
verses.translation: Having understood the command of the great-souled Kubera, I have arrived in your presence; therefore, wait for me without any apprehension.

verses.sanskrit: अदृष्यः सर्वभूतानां सर्वेषां धनदाज्ञया । चराम्यहं प्रभावेण तवाज्ञां परिपालयन् ।। 7.41.10 ।।
verses.translation: By Kubera's command, I am invisible to all beings and I move by my own power, obeying your command.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तदा रामः पुष्पकेण महाबलः । उवाच पुष्पकं दृष्ट्वा विमानं पुनरागतम् ।। 7.41.11 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed by Pushpaka, the mighty Rama then spoke, having seen the Pushpaka aerial car returned again.

verses.sanskrit: यद्येवं स्वागतं ते ऽस्तु विमानवर पुष्पक । आनुकूल्याद्धनेशस्य वृत्तदोषो न नो भवेत् ।। 7.41.12 ।।
verses.translation: If it is so, then welcome to you, O best of aerial cars, Pushpaka; due to the favor of Kubera, no impropriety shall befall us.

verses.sanskrit: लाजैश्चैव तथा पुष्पैर्धूपैश्चैव सुगन्धिभिः । पूजयित्वा महाबाहू राघवः पुष्पकं तदा ।। 7.41.13 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the mighty-armed Rama worshipped the Pushpaka chariot with parched grains, flowers, and fragrant incense.

verses.sanskrit: गम्यतामिति चोवाच आगच्छ त्वं स्मरे यदा । सिद्धानां च गतौ सौम्य मा विषादेन योजय । प्रतिघातश्च ते मा भूद्यथेष्टं गच्छतो दिशः ।। 7.41.14 ।।
verses.translation: Rama then told the Pushpaka, "Depart now, and remember to return whenever I recall you; O gentle one, do not be afflicted with sorrow, and may there be no obstruction to your desired flight in any direction, like that of the Siddhas."

verses.sanskrit: एवमस्त्विति रामेण पूजयित्वा विसर्जितम् । अभिप्रेतां दिशं तस्मात्प्रायात्तत्पुष्पकं तदा ।। 7.41.15 ।।
verses.translation: Having been worshipped and dismissed by Rama with the words "So be it," the Pushpaka then departed from there towards its desired direction.

verses.sanskrit: एवमन्तर्हिते तस्मिन्पुष्पके सुकृतात्मनि । भरतः प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यमुवाच रघुनन्दनम् ।। 7.41.16 ।।
verses.translation: After that Pushpaka, which was of good deeds, had thus disappeared, Bharata, with folded hands, spoke these words to Rama.

verses.sanskrit: विविधात्मनि दृश्यन्ते त्वयि वीर प्रशासति । अमानुषाणां सत्त्वानां व्याहृतानि मुहुर्मुहुः ।। 7.41.17 ।।
verses.translation: O hero, while you rule, the utterances of non-human beings are repeatedly seen in various forms.

verses.sanskrit: अनामयश्च सत्वानां साग्रो मासो गतो ह्ययम् । जीर्णानामपि सत्त्वानां मृत्युर्नायाति राघव ।। 7.41.18 ।।
verses.translation: O Raghava, this full month has passed without any disease among beings, and even for the aged, death does not come.

verses.sanskrit: अरोगप्रसवा नार्यो वपुष्मन्तो हि मानवाः । हर्षश्चाभ्यधिको राजञ्जनस्य पुरवासिनः ।। 7.41.19 ।।
verses.translation: Women give birth without illness, humans are healthy, and O King, the joy of the city dwellers is exceedingly great.

verses.sanskrit: काले वर्षति पर्जन्यः पातयन्नमृतं पयः । वाताश्चापि प्रवान्त्येते स्पर्शयुक्ताः सुखाः शिवाः ।। 7.41.20 ।।
verses.translation: The clouds rain nectar-like water at the proper time, and these winds blow, pleasant to the touch, agreeable, and auspicious.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशो ऽनश्वरो राजा भवेदिति नरेश्वरः । कथयन्ति पुरे राजन्पौरजानपदास्तथा ।। 7.41.21 ।।
verses.translation: O King, the city and country dwellers say in the city, "May such a king be immortal!"

verses.sanskrit: एता वाचः सुमधुरा भरतेन समीरिताः । श्रुत्वा रामो मुदा युक्तो बभूव नृपसत्तमः ।। 7.41.22 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard these very sweet words spoken by Bharata, Rama, the best of kings, became filled with joy. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे एकचत्वारिंशः सर्गः ।। 41 ।। Thus ends the forty-first sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
